message: What was the worst day for quality this week and why?

Looking at the last 7 days of QA-INSP reports: yesterday (2026-05-14) PO-1003 had 6 surface_finish defects (Ra > 1.6 µm) and 3 surface_staining defects out of 52 inspected items, which is the largest single-PO defect cluster of the week. The originating machining ran on 2026-05-13 on CNC-5AX. Pulling the status JSON for that day — CNC-5AX shows a 'degraded' block from 12:45–15:45 with reason 'coolant_quality_degraded'. So the chain is: coolant degraded mid-afternoon Tue → parts machined in that window had marginal surface finish → surfaced at QA-INSP on Wed.
Any MQTT events that day that line up?

Yes — three coolant_temp_high events on cnc-5ax/telemetry: 13:08 (67.2 °C), 14:12 (69.0 °C), 15:30 (66.3 °C). The degraded status block starts at 12:45, so the events are concurrent. Per the coolant spec sheet (sources/coolant-spec-sheet.md), 65 °C is the threshold where surface-finish quality starts degrading measurably. The 13:08 event was the first crossing.
Was the coolant due for a change anyway?

The status JSONs show coolant_changed: false on every day this week. Service life per spec is 120 operating hours; from the past week's status timelines I count ~38 hours of running on CNC-5AX. So the sump was at roughly day 5–6 of the change cycle, not overdue per policy. The trigger here was thermal, not contamination — possible contributors: PO-1003 used Al-7075 Lot B (material-cert-al-7075-lot-B.md), which has hardness right at the spec ceiling and is documented to elevate spindle load ~8 % vs Lot A. More cutting energy → more sump heating.

Two patterns worth noting (no fix proposed, just signal):

1. coolant_temp_high crossing 65 °C is a strong leading indicator for surface-finish defects on the *next* QA-INSP run of those parts. A targeted coolant check at the first 65 °C event — rather than waiting for the calendar-based 120-hour change — would have caught this 2–3 hours earlier.

2. Hardness-margin material lots (Lot B) should be flagged in the production order so the operator knows to watch coolant temp more closely. The lot info is in the material cert but not propagated to the order JSON.

Root cause for PO-1003: coolant_quality_degraded driven by elevated coolant temperature, cause unknown but possibly aggravated by Lot B hardness.
